column1: # OWASP ZAP scan rules — the single source of truth for tuning the DAST scan
# run by .github/workflows/security-dast.yml. Edit this file to silence known
# false positives or to turn a finding into a hard build failure.
# Format (TAB-separated, one rule per line; '#' starts a comment):
#   WARN   — report it, do NOT fail the run (this is the default for every
#            rule, so you never need to add a WARN line; list one only as
#            documentation that the finding is known/accepted).
#   IGNORE — suppress it entirely (confirmed false positive / not applicable).
#   FAIL   — escalate to a build failure. This is the gating knob: the scan
#            runs with `-I`, so ONLY rules marked FAIL here red-light the job.
# Find a rule's ID in the "Plugin ID" column of the HTML/JSON report artifact.
# Examples (uncomment + adjust as you triage real findings):

column3: Timestamp disclosure — epoch-like numbers in JSON, false positive on this API
Suspicious comments — source-map / library noise, not our code
SQL injection — never acceptable, fail the build
Application error disclosure — leaking stack traces, fail the build
